content.text: Now let me check how the README and PLAN docs look and inspect the issue:

content.content:
title:	workouts: import flow can't satisfy the empty-state CTA from a real legacy export
state:	OPEN
author:	patflynn (Patrick Flynn)
labels:	
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	67
--
## Symptom

A user with an export file from the old vanilla-JS Flux app (the only kind of "backup" anyone actually has) cannot use it to satisfy the Workouts tab empty state. The "IMPORT PROGRAM FILE" button rejects the file with:

```
Import failed: Invalid backup: missing or malformed log
```

…even though the file is a valid old-app backup. And separately, even a successful backup import leaves the empty state showing, because the old export format has no `program` field.

## Root cause — three related issues

### 1. Misleading button label

`src/tabs/Workouts/` empty-state CTA says **"IMPORT PROGRAM FILE"**, but the button is wired to `applyImport()` in `logic/exportImport.ts`, which requires the full backup shape:

```ts
if (!data.log || typeof data.log !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data.log)) {
  throw new Error('Invalid backup: missing or malformed log');
}
```

A bare program JSON (just `{meta, phases}`) is rejected. The button accepts only the `{log, state, program?}` envelope.

### 2. Old-app backups have no `program` field

Confirmed by reading the old `exportData()` (`git show bfe9bc4:app.js`):

```js
function exportData() {
  const log = loadLog();
  const state = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STATE_KEY) || '{}');
  const data = { log: log, state: state };  // no program!
  ...
}
```

The old app baked the program into source at `data/program.json` — it was never user data, so the export didn't include it. So:

- A user importing their old backup gets log + state restored, but `program` stays unset → empty state persists → log entries can't be rendered against any program → effectively no migration.

### 3. No path to import only a program

There's also no flow for "I have a program file, I want to load it without touching my log/state." The unified backup format mashes everything together.

## Proposed fix

Split the empty-state CTA from the toolbar's bulk import:

- **Empty-state "Import Program File"** button: accept a bare program JSON (`{meta, phases}`) OR auto-extract the `program` field from a backup envelope. Don't require a `log` field. Migrate exercises by name → catalog id as today.
- **Toolbar "Import"** button (and label): keep handling the full `{log, state, program?}` backup envelope as it does today, but rename to "Import Backup" to make the distinction clear.
- **Old-app backup migration assistant** (stretch): when an `{log, state}`-only file is imported via the toolbar AND no program is loaded in `flux-db`, surface a one-time toast: "Backup imported (history + position). You still need to load a program — use the empty-state Import button on the Workouts tab." Or auto-offer to fetch a default program at first-run.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Empty-state "Import Program File" accepts a bare program JSON and loads it into `flux-db.program`, no `log` field required.
- [ ] Same button also accepts a backup envelope and extracts the `program` field from it (existing behavior subset).
- [ ] Toolbar button renamed "Import Backup" and continues to require the full envelope.
- [ ] If a backup with no `program` field is imported and `flux-db.program` is empty afterward, a clear one-time message tells the user they still need to load a program.
- [ ] Test fixture for the new bare-program import path.

## Out of scope

- Designing the LLM-generated default program flow (PR 5+ in the umbrella plan).
- Shipping a "default" program in the repo. Programs remain runtime data per UMBRELLA-PLAN.md.

## Why now

Without this, the umbrella plan's PR 2 silently breaks the migration path for the one and only userbase (per UMBRELLA-PLAN.md, userbase is 1). Already hit today on PR #66 testing — the workaround was hand-converting `data/program.json` from pre-PR-65 git history into a backup-envelope-wrapped program file, which is not a tenable user experience.

…even though the file is a valid old-app backup. And separately, even a successful backup import leaves the empty state showing, because the old export format has no `program` field.

## Root cause — three related issues

### 1. Misleading button label

`src/tabs/Workouts/` empty-state CTA says **"IMPORT PROGRAM FILE"**, but the button is wired to `applyImport()` in `logic/exportImport.ts`, which requires the full backup shape:

```ts
if (!data.log || typeof data.log !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data.log)) {
  throw new Error('Invalid backup: missing or malformed log');
}
```

A bare program JSON (just `{meta, phases}`) is rejected. The button accepts only the `{log, state, program?}` envelope.

### 2. Old-app backups have no `program` field

Confirmed by reading the old `exportData()` (`git show bfe9bc4:app.js`):

```js
function exportData() {
  const log = loadLog();
  const state = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STATE_KEY) || '{}');
  const data = { log: log, state: state };  // no program!
  ...
}
```

The old app baked the program into source at `data/program.json` — it was never user data, so the export didn't include it. So:

- A user importing their old backup gets log + state restored, but `program` stays unset → empty state persists → log entries can't be rendered against any program → effectively no migration.

### 3. No path to import only a program

There's also no flow for "I have a program file, I want to load it without touching my log/state." The unified backup format mashes everything together.

## Proposed fix

Split the empty-state CTA from the toolbar's bulk import:

- **Empty-state "Import Program File"** button: accept a bare program JSON (`{meta, phases}`) OR auto-extract the `program` field from a backup envelope. Don't require a `log` field. Migrate exercises by name → catalog id as today.
- **Toolbar "Import"** button (and label): keep handling the full `{log, state, program?}` backup envelope as it does today, but rename to "Import Backup" to make the distinction clear.
- **Old-app backup migration assistant** (stretch): when an `{log, state}`-only file is imported via the toolbar AND no program is loaded in `flux-db`, surface a one-time toast: "Backup imported (history + position). You still need to load a program — use the empty-state Import button on the Workouts tab." Or auto-offer to fetch a default program at first-run.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Empty-state "Import Program File" accepts a bare program JSON and loads it into `flux-db.program`, no `log` field required.
- [ ] Same button also accepts a backup envelope and extracts the `program` field from it (existing behavior subset).
- [ ] Toolbar button renamed "Import Backup" and continues to require the full envelope.
- [ ] If a backup with no `program` field is imported and `flux-db.program` is empty afterward, a clear one-time message tells the user they still need to load a program.
- [ ] Test fixture for the new bare-program import path.

## Out of scope

- Designing the LLM-generated default program flow (PR 5+ in the umbrella plan).
- Shipping a "default" program in the repo. Programs remain runtime data per UMBRELLA-PLAN.md.

## Why now

Without this, the umbrella plan's PR 2 silently breaks the migration path for the one and only userbase (per UMBRELLA-PLAN.md, userbase is 1). Already hit today on PR #66 testing — the workaround was hand-converting `data/program.json` from pre-PR-65 git history into a backup-envelope-wrapped program file, which is not a tenable user experience.

file.content:
# Flux

Umbrella app for Workouts, Meditate, and Check-in. Ships as a web PWA at
[flux.gunk.dev](https://flux.gunk.dev) and as a single Android APK with three
launcher icons (one per tab).

See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md` for the plan and `docs/VISION.md` / `docs/DESIGN.md`
for product intent.

## Stack

- Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Capacitor for the native Android shell (`@capacitor/{core,cli,android,app,haptics}`)
- Playwright for e2e tests
- No router, no state library, no UI component library

## Development

All commands run inside `nix develop` (nix is mandatory per `CLAUDE.md`):

```bash
nix develop --command npm ci          # install deps
nix develop --command npm run dev     # Vite dev server on :3030
nix develop --command npm run build   # production build to dist/
```

E2E tests use a separate shell that ships Playwright browsers:

```bash
nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test
```

## Android

```bash
nix develop --command npm run build
npx cap sync android
cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug
```

The Android build requires a host-installed Android SDK + JDK; it is not run
in CI yet.

## Project layout

```
├── index.html              # Vite entry
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx            # Preact entry; reads window.AppEntry bridge
│   ├── App.tsx             # Tab shell
│   ├── tabs/               # Workouts, Meditate, Checkin, Settings
│   ├── llm/                # Provider-agnostic generate() interface
│   └── db/                 # Per-tab IndexedDB stores (added in feature PRs)
├── android/                # Capacitor-scaffolded Android project
├── tests/smoke.spec.ts     # Playwright smoke test
└── capacitor.config.ts
```

gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "workouts: split program import from backup import" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Fixes the Workouts tab empty-state CTA so it can actually load programs from the files users have on hand — bare program JSON and pre-PR-65 backup envelopes both work, and old-app backups no longer leave the user stuck at the empty state with no guidance.

### Three problems, one fix

1. **Empty-state button used the wrong importer.** `IMPORT PROGRAM FILE` was wired to `applyImport()`, which requires the full `{log, state, program?}` envelope. A bare `{meta, phases}` program file was rejected with `Invalid backup: missing or malformed log`.
2. **Old-app backups have no `program` field.** The vanilla-JS `exportData()` only saved `{log, state}` (program was baked into source). After a successful backup import the empty state stayed up because `flux-db.program` was still empty.
3. **No path to import just a program.** The unified backup format mashed everything together.

### What changed

- New `applyProgramImport(raw)` in `src/tabs/Workouts/logic/exportImport.ts`. Sniffs the input shape: bare `{meta, phases}` → migrate and save; envelope with `program` → extract and save; envelope without `program` → throw a helpful error pointing at the toolbar's backup importer. Never touches the log or state stores.
- Empty-state CTA gets its own hidden file input (`import-program-input`) and handler. Errors render inline in the existing `import-message` slot.
- Toolbar button relabeled `Import Backup` (handler unchanged — still `applyImport()`).
- `formatImportMessage` now adds a one-shot hint when a backup is imported with entries but no program, telling the user to use the Workouts tab's `Import Program File` button next.
- New fixtures `program-bare.json` and `backup-no-program.json` drive the new `tests/import.spec.ts` covering all four paths from the issue.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Empty-state "Import Program File" accepts a bare program JSON.
- [x] Same button extracts the `program` field from a backup envelope.
- [x] Toolbar button renamed "Import Backup" and continues to require the full envelope.
- [x] No-program backup → clear one-time message: "You still need to load a program — use Import Program File on the Workouts tab."
- [x] Test fixtures + 4 new tests covering bare program, envelope-with-program, envelope-without-program, and backup-with-history guidance.
- [x] `npm run typecheck` clean.
- [x] `npm run build` clean.
- [x] `npx playwright test` — 13/13 passing.

## Test plan

- [x] Bare `{meta, phases}` JSON loads via the empty-state CTA.
- [x] Backup envelope loads via the empty-state CTA (program only — log unchanged).
- [x] `{log, state}`-only file via the empty-state CTA shows the redirect-to-backup error.
- [x] `{log, state}`-only file via the toolbar Import Backup button restores history and surfaces the "still need to load a program" hint.

Run: 20260518-1400-4bf7cfc9
Fixes #67
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
